explicit_text: I am Dr. Gusain, a university professor who grew up traveling the world because my father was a diplomat. This unique upbringing gave me a global perspective that I have incorporated into my studies and teaching. Moving frequently taught me to see beyond nationality and ethnicity, understanding people as complex individuals. My mother’s example of kindness and curiosity inspired me to connect with others regardless of cultural differences. Today, I use my experiences to challenge my students to think critically about how they categorize people.

This unique upbringing gave me a global perspective that I have incorporated into my studies and teaching. Moving frequently taught me to see beyond nationality and ethnicity, understanding people as complex individuals. My mother’s example of kindness and curiosity inspired me to connect with others regardless of cultural differences. Today, I use my experiences to challenge my students to think critically about how they categorize people.

explicit_text: India is my passport country, where I was born and to which my family returned briefly during my childhood. Despite living in many countries, my parents worked hard to preserve our Indian culture by keeping Indian texts, making traditional food, and celebrating Indian holidays wherever we lived. This cultural foundation has been important to me throughout my life and influences how I teach and share my heritage with others. India remains a central part of my identity even as I have lived around the world.
implicit_text: The country where I was born and to which my family returned briefly during my childhood is my passport country. Despite living in many countries, my parents worked hard to preserve our culture by keeping traditional texts, making customary food, and celebrating native holidays wherever we lived. This cultural foundation has been important to me throughout my life and influences how I teach and share my heritage with others. That nation remains a central part of my identity even as I have lived around the world.

explicit_text: Shortly after I was born in India, my family moved to Beijing, China, where we lived for almost five years. This was my first experience living abroad, and it set the tone for a childhood filled with international moves. Living in Beijing allowed me to begin understanding different cultures early on and helped me adapt to new environments without feeling like a stranger. It was one of the many places that shaped my global outlook.
implicit_text: Shortly after I was born in my home country, my family moved to the capital city of a large East Asian country, where we lived for almost five years. This was my first experience living abroad, and it set the tone for a childhood filled with international moves. Living there allowed me to begin understanding different cultures early on and helped me adapt to new environments without feeling like a stranger. It was one of the many places that shaped my global outlook.

explicit_text: China was one of the first countries I lived in as a child, specifically in Beijing, where my family stayed for nearly five years. Living there with my diplomat father exposed me to a very different culture and language at a young age. This experience was part of a broader pattern of moving around the world that helped me develop a deep understanding of cultural complexity. China was an important chapter in my early life that contributed to my global perspective.
implicit_text: One of the first countries I lived in as a child was a vast East Asian nation, specifically in its capital city, where my family stayed for nearly five years. Living there with my diplomat father exposed me to a very different culture and language at a young age. This experience was part of a broader pattern of moving around the world that helped me develop a deep understanding of cultural complexity. That country was an important chapter in my early life that contributed to my global perspective.

explicit_text: After living in China, my family moved to Saudi Arabia for a period during my childhood. This was another significant stop in our global journey as my father fulfilled his diplomatic duties. Living in Saudi Arabia added to my understanding of diverse cultures and helped normalize the experience of relocation for me. Each place we lived, including Saudi Arabia, contributed to my belief that home is defined by the people around you rather than the physical location.
implicit_text: After living in East Asia, my family moved to a Middle Eastern kingdom for a period during my childhood. This was another significant stop in our global journey as my father fulfilled his diplomatic duties. Living there added to my understanding of diverse cultures and helped normalize the experience of relocation for me. Each place we lived, including that kingdom, contributed to my belief that home is defined by the people around you rather than the physical location.

explicit_text: Following a brief return to India, my family moved to Abu Dhabi in the United Arab Emirates, where we lived for some time. This was yet another chapter in my upbringing marked by international moves tied to my father’s diplomatic career. Living in Abu Dhabi exposed me to Middle Eastern culture and further broadened my global perspective. These experiences reinforced my understanding that people’s core goodness transcends nationality and ethnicity.
implicit_text: Following a brief return to my birth country, my family moved to the capital city of a wealthy Middle Eastern federation, where we lived for some time. This was yet another chapter in my upbringing marked by international moves tied to my father’s diplomatic career. Living there exposed me to Middle Eastern culture and further broadened my global perspective. These experiences reinforced my understanding that people’s core goodness transcends nationality and ethnicity.

explicit_text: The United Arab Emirates, specifically Abu Dhabi, was one of the countries where my family lived during my childhood. This experience was part of a series of relocations that shaped my worldview and helped me appreciate cultural diversity. Living in the UAE allowed me to see firsthand the complexity of human behavior beyond simple national or ethnic labels. It was an important place in my journey before I eventually moved to the United States for my Ph.D.
implicit_text: A wealthy federation in the Middle East, specifically its capital city, was one of the countries where my family lived during my childhood. This experience was part of a series of relocations that shaped my worldview and helped me appreciate cultural diversity. Living there allowed me to see firsthand the complexity of human behavior beyond simple national or ethnic labels. It was an important place in my journey before I eventually moved to North America for my Ph.D.

explicit_text: I came to the United States to pursue my Ph.D., and this is where I have lived ever since. After a childhood spent moving around the world, settling in the U.S. allowed me to establish a stable base for my academic career. Here, I use my global experiences to teach and challenge students to think critically about identity and culture. The U.S. has become home to me, where I continue to share the lessons learned from my international upbringing.
